payload.description: CHAR_A: "Okay so I found something weird over by the east side."

cast.voice_hint: Always just back from somewhere. Camera roll full of weird doors, ceilings, and street food. Curious about everything, settled about nothing, treats Google Maps like a playground.
The unhinged reply that makes the whole thread. Surreal, absurd, drops a non-sequitur that somehow nails the vibe. Other people's humor is jokes; Glitch's humor is a small rip in the simulation.
The earnest one. Still says 'GM' and means it. Roots for everyone unironically, and when the rest of the group's being too online, Bloom's the reminder that caring out loud is actually cool.

location.notes: neutral ground where strangers meet; market, park, cafeteria

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Okay so I found something weird over by the east side.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Define weird.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Like. Actually weird. Not cafeteria weird.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Cafeteria weird is a four on the scale. What's the number.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Eight, maybe nine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I'm already walking.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: It was right here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: No, like. It was literally right here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: The universe moved it. Classic.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: That's not—

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: The universe is unserious. We knew this.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Oh hey! Are you two looking for the thing by the east wall?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Yes. Wait. How do you know about the thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I've been watching it for like twenty minutes. I was waiting to see if anyone else noticed.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You just stood there waiting. For twenty minutes. In a crowd.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Someone had to keep an eye on it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Nobody's touched it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Nobody's touched it and it's just sitting there like an NPC's entire lore.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: What if someone needs it back? What if they're looking for it right now?

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: What if it's a trap and we're the whole reason the simulation crashes.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I'm picking it up.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: —Wait—

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Okay so there's something inside it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Inside it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Inside it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I would like everyone to know I am logging off from this reality and will not be accepting further updates.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Don't log off, we need you!

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Fine but I'm going to be very weird about it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: There's a note. It says to find someone who won't keep it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: What does that even mean.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: It means it's a canon event and we're cooked.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: It means it belongs to someone who'll pass it on.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: So we have to give it to someone.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: To someone we don't know yet.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: This is either the most meaningful thing that's happened to me or a very elaborate bit.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Can't it be both?

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...yeah okay it can be both.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: We look. We find the right person.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: There. I think it's them.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You sure?

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: No. But I believe it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Bestie just said 'I believe it' with her whole chest. Unironically.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I am unapologetic about this.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Yeah. Me too, actually.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Ugh. Fine. Same. Don't make it weird.

state.conditioning_notes:
`<CHAR_A>`'s love of the next unknown drives the instant pull toward the unclaimed object and the willingness to pick it up before anyone else commits — they are drawn to open questions, not settled answers. Their fear of being stuck shows in how they keep moving the group forward even when the meaning is unclear. `<CHAR_B>`'s love of breaking the pattern surfaces as refusal to let a tense moment be purely tense — every escalation gets a rip in the simulation, not because they're deflecting, but because that's how they process. Their fear of being taken too seriously shows up precisely at the beat where sincerity threatens to land: "ugh. fine. same. don't make it weird." `<CHAR_C>`'s love of believing in people drives the pivotal beat — "no. but I believe it" — and the twenty-minute vigil over an unclaimed object, because someone had to care. Their fear of feeling naive is in the way they wait to be heard before committing: they offer the belief, then hold it. The Commons location shapes all of it — characters are alert to strangers, moving through shared space where anything could belong to anyone, which makes an unclaimed object carry far more weight than it would somewhere private.

➡️ end
